name: Pod Status Analyzer
description: Quickly diagnose common pod failure states including CrashLoopBackOff, ImagePullBackOff, Pending, and OOMKilled.

steps.title: Get pod status with restart count
steps.description: View the current status and restart count for pods.

steps.title: Check why a pod is Pending
steps.description: View scheduling events to understand why a pod cannot be placed on a node.
steps.tip: Common causes: insufficient resources, node affinity/taint issues, or PVC binding failures.

steps.title: Diagnose CrashLoopBackOff
steps.description: View logs from the crashed container to understand the failure.
steps.tip: The --previous flag shows logs from the last crashed instance, which is where the error usually is.

steps.title: Check for OOMKilled containers
steps.description: Find containers that were killed due to exceeding their memory limit.
steps.warning: OOMKilled means the container exceeded its memory limit. Increase the limit or optimize memory usage.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Pod Status Analyzer
set -euo pipefail

NS=${1:-"default"}

echo "=== Pod Status Summary ($NS) ==="
kubectl get pods -n "$NS" -o custom-columns='NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.containerStatuses[0].ready,STATUS:.status.phase,RESTARTS:.status.containerStatuses[0].restartCount,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp'

echo ""
echo "=== Problem Pods ==="
echo "--- CrashLoopBackOff ---"
kubectl get pods -n "$NS" -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.status.containerStatuses[]?.state.waiting.reason=="CrashLoopBackOff") | .metadata.name' 2>/dev/null || echo "None"

echo "--- ImagePullBackOff ---"
kubectl get pods -n "$NS" -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.status.containerStatuses[]?.state.waiting.reason=="ImagePullBackOff" or .status.containerStatuses[]?.state.waiting.reason=="ErrImagePull") | .metadata.name' 2>/dev/null || echo "None"

echo "--- Pending ---"
kubectl get pods -n "$NS" --field-selector=status.phase=Pending -o name 2>/dev/null || echo "None"

echo "--- OOMKilled ---"
kubectl get pods -n "$NS" -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.status.containerStatuses[]?.lastState.terminated.reason=="OOMKilled") | .metadata.name' 2>/dev/null || echo "None"

faq.question: What is the difference between Pending and ContainerCreating?
faq.answer: Pending means the pod is waiting to be scheduled to a node. ContainerCreating means it is scheduled but the container runtime is still pulling the image or setting up volumes.

faq.question: How do I fix ImagePullBackOff?
faq.answer: Check: (1) the image name and tag are correct, (2) the image exists in the registry, (3) image pull secrets are configured if the registry requires authentication.
